new_classes.label_en: Wine with PGI in containers > 2 L with actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol (CN 2204 29 79)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen > 2 L mit einem vorhandenen Alkoholgehalt > 15 % vol (KN 2204 29 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of more than 2 litres nominal volume, with an actual alcoholic strength exceeding 15% vol, classified under CN 2204 29 79.

coverage_explanation: The three primary distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) PGI quality via someValuesFrom PGIOriginQuality, (2) large container (> 2 L) via someValuesFrom LargeContainer (which equivalently models ≥ 2 L — the CN uses > 2 L for this subheading, covered well enough by the existing LargeContainer class which starts at ≥ 2 L), and (3) actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol via a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. Coverage is reduced to 0.75 because: (a) the reference to additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 (which specifies the precise PGI definition criteria) cannot be fully modelled without the text of that note; (b) the sibling CN code 22042980 is mentioned in the same legal note, indicating these two codes are co-defined, but no disjointness axiom between them is proposed here as 22042980 has not yet been processed; and (c) the LargeContainer class uses ≥ 2 L (minInclusive 2.0) rather than strictly > 2 L, creating a minor boundary imprecision at exactly 2 L.
